arch_opening: Three Man Team v2.0.0 is available — this is a major change. We've ported the entire framework from Claude Code to opencode. I'll walk you through what that means for your setup. This will take a few minutes, but it's worth doing right.

changes.title: Ported from Claude Code to opencode
changes.what_changed: The entire framework now runs on opencode instead of Claude Code. Agent definitions moved from root-level ARCHITECT.md/BUILDER.md/REVIEWER.md to .opencode/agents/*.md or ~/.config/opencode/agents/*.md with YAML frontmatter. Config replaced: CLAUDE.md removed, replaced by opencode.json and .opencode/ directory structure.
changes.old_behavior: Agents defined as root-level markdown files loaded by CLAUDE.md. Builder and Reviewer spun up via Claude Code's Agent tool.
changes.new_behavior: Agents defined as .opencode/agents/*.md or ~/.config/opencode/agents/*.md files with frontmatter (mode, model, description). Architect is a primary agent, Builder and Reviewer are subagents. Delegation uses opencode's Task tool. Global install uses opencode's deep-merge — agents at ~/.config/opencode/ are available in all projects.
changes.user_impact_plain: Your entire setup changes. You'll need to delete your old ARCHITECT.md, BUILDER.md, REVIEWER.md files and CLAUDE.md, then copy the new project template files and optionally install agents globally. The handoff/ folder stays the same.
changes.how_to_assess: Check if the user still has root-level ARCHITECT.md, BUILDER.md, REVIEWER.md, and CLAUDE.md in their project. If they do, those need to be removed and replaced with the new template structure.
changes.user_decision: This is a required upgrade — the old Claude Code structure will not work with opencode. Would you like me to walk you through the migration?

changes.title: Template architecture redesigned
changes.what_changed: Replaced the old project-folder/generic duplicate templates with a two-layer system: templates/global/ (agents, skills, opencode.json for ~/.config/opencode/) and templates/project/ (handoff state, project opencode.json, VERSION, new-setup.md, PROJECT.md). opencode deep-merges global config into every project automatically.
changes.old_behavior: Two complete template copies (project-folder and generic) that differed only in persona content. Both were 'copy everything into the project' approaches. Neither used opencode's global config merge system.
changes.new_behavior: templates/global/ provides reusable infrastructure (agent definitions, skill, agent config) that installs at ~/.config/opencode/ for cross-project use. templates/project/ provides project-specific state (handoff/, opencode.json with default_agent + instructions only, VERSION, PROJECT.md). Per-project install copies both into the project's .opencode/ directory.
changes.user_impact_plain: If you used the old generic template for global install, delete it and use templates/global/ instead. It now properly leverages opencode's deep-merge system. If you used project-folder, the equivalent is now templates/project/ — but agent files are in templates/global/ (copied into .opencode/ for per-project or ~/.config/opencode/ for global).
changes.how_to_assess: Check if the user has old project-folder/ or generic/ template files. Walk them through which files to keep (handoff/ is unchanged) and which to replace (agent files and opencode.json split differently now).
changes.user_decision: Do you want per-project install (agents in .opencode/) or global install (agents in ~/.config/opencode/)? Global install means agents are available in all projects automatically.

changes.title: CLAUDE.md replaced by two opencode.json files
changes.what_changed: The session router is now split across two opencode.json files: global (agent definitions + skills, no default_agent or instructions) and project (default_agent + instructions only, no agent defs). opencode deep-merges them.
changes.old_behavior: CLAUDE.md was a single ~38-line markdown file that routed every session. It referenced @.claude/skills/token-optimization.md.
changes.new_behavior: Global opencode.json defines agents and skills paths. Project opencode.json sets default_agent and project-specific instructions. opencode deep-merges project config over global config. Instructions arrays are concatenated and deduplicated.
changes.user_impact_plain: You'll need to delete your old CLAUDE.md. If you had custom content in CLAUDE.md, that content should move to an instructions file referenced in the project-level opencode.json. For global install, agent definitions go in ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json — NOT default_agent or instructions.
changes.how_to_assess: Read the user's existing CLAUDE.md. If it has custom project content beyond the Three Man Team router, that content needs to be preserved in a separate file referenced by project-level opencode.json instructions.
changes.user_decision: Does your current CLAUDE.md have custom content that needs to be preserved? If so, what file should I put it in?

changes.title: Token-optimizer skill moved
changes.what_changed: The token-optimizer skill moved from .claude/skills/token-optimization.md to .opencode/skills/token-optimization/SKILL.md (per-project) or ~/.config/opencode/skills/token-optimization/SKILL.md (global).
changes.old_behavior: Skill was a single markdown file loaded via @.claude/skills/ reference in CLAUDE.md.
changes.new_behavior: Skill is now a SKILL.md file in its own directory. For per-project install: .opencode/skills/token-optimization/SKILL.md. For global install: ~/.config/opencode/skills/token-optimization/SKILL.md. Auto-loaded via opencode.json skills.paths configuration or opencode's global skill discovery.
changes.user_impact_plain: If you customized the token-optimizer skill, you'll need to copy your changes to the new location. Global install puts it at ~/.config/opencode/skills/ so it works in all projects.
changes.how_to_assess: Read the user's existing .claude/skills/token-optimization.md if it exists. Compare with the new SKILL.md template. If the user made customizations, those need to be migrated.
changes.user_decision: Did you customize the token-optimizer skill? If so, I'll help you migrate those changes.

changes.title: Model IDs use provider prefix format
changes.what_changed: Model IDs now use opencode's provider-prefix format: anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 instead of claude-sonnet-4-6. Model overrides go in the project-level opencode.json, which deep-merges over the global agent definitions.
changes.old_behavior: Claude Code used bare model IDs like claude-opus-4-7.
changes.new_behavior: opencode uses provider/model format like anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6, openai/gpt-4o, etc. Override models per-project in the project-level opencode.json agent key — it deep-merges over global definitions.
changes.user_impact_plain: If you configured specific models per agent, the model IDs need updating. This is a simple format change — just add the provider prefix. Model overrides go in the project opencode.json, not the global one.
changes.how_to_assess: Read the user's project opencode.json. Find any model references. Update them to provider-prefix format.
changes.user_decision: Which provider and model do you want for each agent? The defaults are: Architect inherits session model, Builder uses anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6, Reviewer uses anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6.
